2605.02488.title: Efficient Temporal Datalog Materialisation for Composite Event Recognition
2605.02488.paper_summary: Several applications demand the timely detection of critical situations, such as threats to safety and transparency, over high-velocity streams of symbolic events. This demand has motivated the development of (i) event specification languages, which define composite events via temporal patterns over simpler events, and (ii) stream reasoning frameworks, evaluating patterns expressed in these languages. However, event specification languages are typically studied in isolation, complicating their comparison in terms of expressivity and obscuring the scope of their associated stream reasoners. To mitigate this issue, we map practical fragments of prominent event specification languages into Temporal Datalog->-, a temporal Datalog with stratified negation and no future dependencies. To support efficient stream reasoning over Temporal Datalog->-, we propose Streaming Trigger Graphs, an extension of a state-of-the-art technique for Datalog materialisation. Our approach yields a uniform composite event recognition mechanism that has the potential to generalise across a wide range of practical event specification languages.
2605.02488.paper_summary_zh: 幾個應用需求及時檢測關鍵情況，例如對安全性和透明度的威脅，這些情況發生在高速流動的符號事件中。這一需求促使了（i）事件規範語言的發展，這些語言通過簡單事件的時間模式定義復合事件，以及（ii）流推理框架，評估用這些語言表達的模式。然而，事件規範語言通常是孤立研究的，這使得它們在表達能力上的比較變得複雜，並模糊了其相關流推理器的範疇。為了減輕這一問題，我們將一些著名事件規範語言的實用片段映射到Temporal Datalog->，這是一種具有分層否定且無未來依賴的時間Datalog。為了支持對Temporal Datalog->的高效流推理，我們提出了流觸發圖，這是對Datalog物化的最先進技術的擴展。我們的方法產生了一種統一的復合事件識別機制，具有在廣泛的實用事件規範語言中進行概括的潛力。
2605.02488.author: Periklis Mantenoglou et.al.
